Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Bumrah or Hazlewood, doesn't matter: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi says 'play the ball, not bowler'
Article Text:
Photo/IPL

Watch Yashasvi Jaiswal reveals RR dressing room mood after third straight win Yashasvi Jaiswal reveals RR dressing room mood after third straight win

The other day it was Jasprit Bumrah . On Friday, Josh Hazlewood faced the same treatment from the wonder kid Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, as the 15-year-old said that he "plays the ball" and "not the bowler".Sooryavanshi scored 78 off 26 balls as Rajasthan Royals (RR) chased down Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s (RCB) total of 201 with two overs remaining.He also moved to the top of the IPL 2026 Orange Cap list with 200 runs in four matches.Speaking at the post-match presentation after RR’s win, Sooryavanshi said he focuses on his natural game and what he practises, instead of trying anything extra, and reacts to the ball rather than the bowler."I try to execute what I practice and back my natural game. Yes, at the back of your mind, you know who the bowler (Bumrah or Hazlewood) is but you play the ball not the bowler," said Sooryavanshi, who hit the Australian pacer for three boundaries and a six in four consecutive deliveries.After gaining attention early in his career, Sooryavanshi said he remains focused, with guidance from his father Sanjiv and RR support staff member Romi Bhinder."They always tell me that it is a long journey and my focus should only and only be on the game," he said.He also said he was not satisfied after getting out on 78, as he felt he could have added more runs for the team."My thinking is that had I been around, I could have scored 20 more runs which would be beneficial for the team," Sooryavanshi said at the post-match presentation ceremony.Rajasthan Royals defeated Royal Challengers Bengaluru by six wickets, extending their unbeaten run to four matches and handing RCB their first loss of the season.Chasing 202, RR were led by Sooryavanshi’s 78 and an unbeaten 81 from Dhruv Jurel. The two added 108 runs, while Ravindra Jadeja remained not out on 24 as RR reached the target in 18 overs.Earlier, RCB made 201 for 8, with captain Rajat Patidar scoring 63 and Venkatesh Iyer contributing late. Jofra Archer and Ravi Bishnoi took early wickets, but RCB still crossed 200, which was not enough against RR’s batting.
Article Summary: Sooryavanshi scored 78 off 26 balls as rajasthan royals (rr) chased down royal challengers bengaluru’s (rcb) total of 201. He also moved to the top of the ipl 2026 orange cap list with 200 runs in four matches. Rr extended their unbeaten run to four matches and handed rcb their first loss of the season.

Article Title: Mukul Choudhary: 36 sixes in an innings, 500 km bus rides, an MS Dhoni dream
Article Text:
Mukul Choudhary (BCCI Photo)

36 SIXES IN AN INNINGS



MUKUL CHOUDHARY: NOT JUST ANOTHER HITTER



ULTIMATE DREAM - MEETING DHONI



Lucknow Super Giants will next face Chennai Super Kings on May 10.



NEW DELHI: Calm under pressure and fearless at the death, Mukul Choudhary lit up Eden Gardens with a 27-ball 57, launching seven sixes to script a remarkable last-over win for Lucknow Super Giants against Kolkata Knight Riders on Thursday.The emotions were written all over the 21-year-old from Jhunjhunu’s face as he struck the winning runs - holding his nerve, battling anxiety, negotiating yorkers in the final over from Vaibhav Arora, waiting for the right ball to send over the ropes, and then raising both arms in celebration before his teammates sprinted in from the dugout to mob him.That six-hitting IPL night, however, is backed by an even bigger story of preparation. This wasn’t the first time the youngster from Sikar, Jhunjhunu, Rajasthan had taken bowlers apart. Long before making headlines in the IPL, he had already smashed an incredible 36 sixes in an innings.In Sikar (Jhunjhunu), where Mukul trains, he practices on black soil. Adjusting to red-soil wickets, however, became a major challenge. That’s when he turned to his Under-23 batting coach Chandrapal Singh. In Rajasthan, red-soil pitches are found in Udaipur and Nathdwara.The distance was daunting, but an adamant Mukul embraced the challenge.Packing his kit at night, he would board a bus to Udaipur (in Rajasthan) - and sometimes Nathdwara (in Rajasthan) - both nearly 500 km from Jhunjhunu."Mukul is extremely hardworking. I didn’t know if he would agree to it, but he accepted the challenge despite all the travel. He had to cover long distances - it’s not easy to travel 500 km by bus, play a match, stay overnight, and then return. He did this almost three times a week," Chandrapal told TimesofIndia.com."He once hit 36 sixes in a T20 match. We had called up a couple of teams, and Mukul’s side was up against them. I knew he would play a big knock, but I didn’t expect him to set things on fire the way he did. He was hitting the ball brutally. He has also practiced MS Dhoni’s helicopter shot,” Chandrapal said.“You will find red soil in places like Mumbai, Chennai, Ahmedabad, Cuttack and Karnataka. The way he stepped out of his comfort zone to prepare for this IPL is remarkable. He is a complete team man—team always comes first for him,” he added.Mukul enjoyed an impressive run in domestic cricket.After making a mark at the Under-23 level, he scored 173 runs in five innings in the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy 2025–26 at a striking rate of 198.85. His superb form with the bat earned him an IPL contract, with Lucknow Super Giants picking him up for Rs 2.60 crore at the IPL 2026 auction.For the 21-year-old, the ability to clear the ropes with ease - from bat swing to footwork, reach, and reading the situation - has not come overnight.“I have practised that shot since childhood,” Mukul had said during the post-match press conference after pulling off the heist.“My body is naturally a bit powerful, and that has helped me. I also practise hitting 100–150 sixes every day, so the bat speed develops if you keep repeating it. I have been training hard for the last five to six months, and it has now become part of my game,” he had said.After the match, LSG captain Rishabh Pant, who had looked tense until the final delivery, appeared visibly relieved. A broad smile spread across his face as he walked up to hug Mukul.For Chandrapal, Mukul has always been a player who puts the team above everything else.“More than just a classy batter, he is a brilliant team man. His first priority is always the team. I noticed this when he first came to me. In every situation, he would ask, ‘Sir, how can I help the team win?’ That attitude has made him special and brought him this far,” he said.“We have seen players like Russell and Pollard, who are physically very strong hitters. He is similar in that sense. He has that kind of muscular build and a very different mindset. If he has to hit 100 sixes in a day, he can do it comfortably,” added Chandrapal.Among his seven sixes against Kolkata Knight Riders on Saturday night, one stood out - a Dhoni-esque helicopter shot that sent the Eden Gardens crowd into a deafening roar. The entire LSG dugout, including Rishabh Pant, was left stunned.Lucknow Super Giants now will next face Chennai Super Kings on May 10 and for Mukul Choudhary, he will not just be riding on the high of his incredible innings, he will be looking forward to meeting his idol MS Dhoni."I always look up to MS Dhoni because I am also a finisher. I always look up to him. His helicopter shot, which is very iconic, is my favourite. The way he led India in the 2011 World Cup, everybody remembers it. I want to be like him and finish off matches and help my team win," Mukul had said on JioHotstar after the game.Before that, their schedule is packed: they take on Gujarat Titans on Sunday, followed by Royal Challengers Bengaluru on April 15, Punjab Kings on April 19, Rajasthan Royals on April 22, Kolkata Knight Riders on April 26, Mumbai Indians on May 4, and once again Royal Challengers Bengaluru on May 7.
Article Summary: Mukul choudhary hit seven sixes in last-over win over kolkata knight riders. The 21-year-old from jhunjhunu, rajasthan had already hit 36 sixes. He trains on black soil in rajasththan, but red soil wickets are found in udaipur. Mukul choudhary hit seven sixes against kolkata knight riders.

Article Title: Drama in PSL over Usman Tariq's action: Batter walks away twice vs Pakistan spinner - Watch
Article Text:
Photo/screengrab

Watch How Justin Langer’s belief helped Mukul Choudhary shine How Justin Langer’s belief helped Mukul Choudhary shine

The Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2026 saw a fresh controversy on Friday in a match between the Quetta Gladiators and the Rawalpindiz, as Daryl Mitchell pulled out twice during Usman Tariq’s bowling pause.The New Zealand batter raised concerns about the long pause in Tariq’s action before release. Mitchell walked away twice during the over, before the umpires stepped in to allow play to continue.The incident took place during the match between Quetta Gladiators and Rawalpindi Pindiz. Facing Tariq in the ninth over, Mitchell played a paddle sweep off the first ball he faced.However, he appeared uncomfortable with the pause in the bowler’s action. He walked away twice, pointing towards the mid-action pause. The umpires then intervened and spoke to the batter before play resumed.Tariq conceded three singles off the first three balls of the over. Before the fourth delivery, Mitchell walked away at the last moment due to the delay. The umpire approached him after repeated instances and had a brief conversation before the game continued.Mitchell was later dismissed, caught and bowled by Saud Shakeel, as Pindiz lost the match by 61 runs in Karachi.Mitchell’s approach is linked to advice from Ravichandran Ashwin. Tariq had been a talking point during the T20 World Cup earlier this year, with Ashwin suggesting that batters should walk away, which would force the umpire to call a dead ball. By doing so, Ashwin believes the batter can shift the delay back onto the bowler and umpire, and reduce any advantage gained from disrupting timing.Tariq has been under scrutiny for his stop-start run-up and action. His run-up is followed by a pause before release, after which he goes down on his knees to complete the delivery.He has been reported twice for a suspect action and cleared on both occasions.
Article Summary: The incident took place during the match between quetta gladiators and rawalpindi pindiz. Daryl mitchell walked away twice during usman tariq's bowling pause. The umpires stepped in to allow play to continue.

Article Title: Chiefs Vikings Trade: Kansas City Chiefs $9M defensive end linked to Minnesota Vikings $42M outside linebacker
Article Text:
Kansas City Chiefs $9M defensive end linked to Minnesota Vikings $42M outside linebacker (Image via Getty: Kansas City Chiefs logo and Minnesota Vikings logo)

NFL Trade Rumors : Chiefs edge Felix Anudike-Uzomah tied to Vikings OLB Jonathan Greenard



Minnesota edge could ask for meaningful raise in Kansas City



Jimmy Johnson chart for Chiefs and Vikings trade

Brian Flores could eye Mansoor Delane as 9th overall or Caleb Downs as Harrison Smith replacement

Jonathan Greenard’s departure would give Dallas Turner chance

With just weeks remaining until the 2026 NFL Draft, speculation around picks and subsequent trade packages has started blossoming like no spring this offseason! In light of this, ESPN’s Bill Barnwell suggests an interesting trade package involving both NFC and AFC teams: The Kansas City Chiefs and the Minnesota Vikings.The Chiefs get picks in the first and third rounds, 18th overall and 97th overall, along with the Vikings outside linebacker Jonathan Greenard. Minnesota acquires the first-round 9th overall pick along with Kansas City defensive end Felix Anudike-Uzomah. Now, how do they make sense and fit into each other’s puzzle? Let’s decipher.Kansas City DE George Karlaftis was a first-round 30th overall pick, and DT Chris Jones was a second-round 37th overall pick. Both have proved themselves over the years. Karlaftis settled as a solid starter, and Jones’ impact has not dipped as much as his stats production suggests.Barnwell suggests that the Chiefs could not land an edge rusher at 9th overall in the first round. But if they were to think of acquiring Minnesota’s OLB Jonathan Greenard, they’d have a couple of years before worrying about finding a good edge rusher.Greenard recorded 59 total tackles, 12.0 sacks, 22 QB hits, 18 tackles for loss, four forced fumbles, and three passes defended in his 2024 campaign.He was selected to the Pro Bowl that season. Injuries plagued the final games of his 2025 season, but in the 10 games that he started, he recorded 38 combined tackles, 3.0 sacks, and one forced fumble.He will turn 29 this May, but he will have a few impactful pass-rushing years before Kansas City chases for another player.As for Greenard’s contract, the Florida alum signed a four-year, $76M contract in 2024. He still has two years and $37M left on his deal. In 2026, he is scheduled to earn an $18M base salary and a $100K workout bonus while carrying a $22M cap hit and a $13M dead cap value. His total career earnings are $42M.Chiefs GM Brett Veach may have to offer a meaningful raise, as Greenard could be a “justifiable addition” to the perennial Super Bowl contenders. 2025 was the only season they did not make it to the playoffs in over a decade! Anyhow, a pass-rushing boost may give them the hope they need for the 2026 chapter.As for Anudike-Uzomah’s contract details: he signed a four-year, $11M contract with Kansas City in 2023. He is scheduled to earn a $2M base salary while carrying a $3.7M cap hit and a $1.3M dead cap value in 2026. His total career earnings are $9M.As for the Vikings’ picture? Barnwell states that, as per the Jimmy Johnson chart, the difference between Greenard and Anudike-Uzomah is worth the 54th pick in a typical draft. Greenard is a proven productive starter, whereas Anudike-Uzomah is a newbie struggling to leave an impact.Their difference is 54th overall pick, which is equivalent to a mid-second-round selection.Long story short: they would move from 18th overall pick to premier 9th overall pick, thereby getting a chance to acquire one of the best defensive backs in this draft class.Barnwell predicts LSU Tigers cornerback Mansoor Delane for Minnesota under Brian Flores’ reign as defensive coordinator.The idea of Ohio State Buckeyes safety Caleb Downs in a Flores defense sounds “tantalizing” to Barnwell, but if that happens, he’d be the perfect Harrison Smith replacement because he can excel both in run defense and coverage just like Smith. The veteran was released on March 11.As for the rest of the Vikings players: If Greenard’s not there, it would create an opening in the starting lineup for Dallas Turner. Turner stepped up when Greenard and Andrew Van Ginkel were injured last year.Turner is a 2024 first-round pick. He recorded 42 total pressures as a pass rusher [9 sacks, 27 hurries, and 6 hits]. He also recorded 35 solo tackles with four forced fumbles.His overall PFF grade was 65.6 in 2025, which ranked him 62nd among 115 qualified edge defenders. His PFF pass-rush grade was 70.2, which ranked him 38th among others, and his PFF run-defense grade was 66.9, which also ranked him 38th among others.So, would the Chiefs and the Vikings consider this trade package? Remains to be seen…
Article Summary: Kansas city chiefs $9m defensive end linked to minnesota vikings $42m outside linebacker. Minnesota acquires the first-round 9th overall pick along with kansas city defensive end felix anudike-uzomah. The chiefs get picks in the first and third rounds, 18th overall and 97th overall, along with the vikings outside linebacker jonathan greenard.

NEW DELHI: Kolkata Knight Riders’ young batter Angkrish Raghuvanshi climbed to third place in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap standings. The right-hander has amassed 155 runs from four matches, combining fluency with consistency to emerge as one of KKR’s standout performers this season.His teammate and skipper Ajinkya Rahane has also made notable progress, climbing to fifth in the run charts with 124 runs in four games. Rahane’s composed approach at the top has complemented Raghuvanshi’s strokeplay, giving KKR a solid batting foundation.At the top of the leaderboard, Yashasvi Jaiswal continues to hold the Orange Cap with 170 runs in three matches.The Rajasthan Royals opener has been in sublime touch, scoring at a brisk strike rate of 163.46 while maintaining remarkable consistency.Close on his heels is Sameer Rizvi of Delhi Capitals, who occupies the second spot with 160 runs at a strike rate of 161.62, showcasing his aggressive intent.Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Heinrich Klaasen sits fourth with 145 runs, continuing his reputation as a destructive middle-order batter. His ability to accelerate in crucial phases has made him a key figure in SRH’s batting line-up.The competition for the Orange Cap remains intense, with several batters not far behind. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has impressed with his fearless approach, scoring 122 runs at a staggering strike rate of 248.98, while Rohit Sharma and Jos Buttler continue to provide consistency at the top.Among others, Devdutt Padikkal, Shubman Gill and Virat Kohli have also made strong starts, ensuring the race remains wide open as the tournament progresses.
Article Summary: Yashasvi jaiswal continues to hold the orange cap with 170 runs in three matches. Rajasthan royals opener has been in sublime touch, scoring at a brisk strike rate of 163.46. Angkrish raghuvanshi climbed to third place in the ipl 2026 orange cap standings.

NEW DELHI: Lucknow Super Giants moved up to fifth spot in the IPL 2026 Points Table after their win over the Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) on Thursday. LSG now have 4 points, with two wins in three matches with an NRR of -0.359.KKR, meanwhile, continue to remain in ninth place with just one point from four games which came in the washout against Punjab Kings, and are still searching for their first win of the season.Unbeaten Rajasthan Royals remain at the top of the IPL 2026 Points Table with 3 wins in 3 matches accumulating 6 points with a NRR of 2.403, followed by Punjab Kings in second place with 5 points (2 wins in 3 matches, 1 N/R with NRR of 0.637). Royal Challengers Bengaluru are in third place with 4 points (2 wins in 2 matches with NRR of 2.501). Delhi Capitals are placed at number four with 4 points. They have two wins in three games.SRH are placed sixth with 2 points (1 win in 3 matches with NRR of 0.275), followed by Mumbai Indians at 8th spot with 2 points (1 win in 3 matches with NRR of -0.715) and KKR at 9th place with 1 point (0 wins, 1 N/R with NRR of -1.964). The last place, 10th, is occupied by CSK who are yet to open their account, enduring three defeats in as many games (NRR -2.517).Lucknow Super Giants edged past Kolkata Knight Riders by three wickets in a tight IPL 2026 clash at Eden Gardens, boosting their position in the points table. Chasing 182, LSG rode on strong knocks from Ayush Badoni and Mukul Choudhary to seal the win, while KKR’s winless run continued.Earlier, KKR posted 181/4 with contributions from Ajinkya Rahane, Angkrish Raghuvanshi, Cameron Green and Rovman Powell. However, their bowling couldn’t hold the total as LSG chased it down in the final over.
Article Summary: Unbeaten rajasthan royals remain at the top of the ipl 2026 points table. Punjab kings in second place with 5 points (2 wins in 3 matches, 1 n/r with nrr of 0.637) royal challengers bengaluru are in third place with 4 points. Delhi capitals are placed at number four with four points.

Article Title: RR vs RCB, IPL Match Preview: Can Royal Challengers keep Sooryavanshi in check?
Article Text:
RCB skipper Rajat Patidar with Virat Kohli; and RR's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Watch Yashasvi Jaiswal reveals RR dressing room mood after third straight win Yashasvi Jaiswal reveals RR dressing room mood after third straight win

Poll Who do you think is the standout player for Rajasthan Royals this season? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Yashasvi Jaiswal Shane Bond

NUMBER GAMES

4 - Ten-wicket wins for RCB, the most in the IPL . Two of those have come against Rajasthan Royals, in Bengaluru in 2010 and at the Wankhede in 2021.

- Ten-wicket wins for RCB, the most in the . Two of those have come against Rajasthan Royals, in Bengaluru in 2010 and at the Wankhede in 2021. 113 - Virat Kohli’s unbeaten 113 off 72 balls in Jaipur on April 6, 2024 is the highest individual score in RR-RCB matches.

GUWAHATI: All discussions about Rajasthan Royals ’ highflying start to the season have centred on a certain 15-year-old phenomenon, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The eve of Royals’ third and last home match this season here at their ‘second home ground’ was not different either, even with the in-form defending champions Royals Challengers Bengaluru (RCB), inspired by King Virat Kohli , coming to the city for the first time.Royals’ fast bowling coach Shane Bond was full of praise for the teen prodigy on Thursday. “He’s incredible. I have a 17-year-old son and when I compare him to Vaibhav, what he’s doing is remarkable,” Bond said. Highlighting his maturity level and developing game awareness, the former Kiwi pacer added, “It’s not just reckless hitting, he’s picking the right balls and showing growth. There’s a maturity in his batting. ”While the boy wonder has expectedly translated his flamboyance into match-defining knocks up front, his ‘senior’ opening partner Yashasvi Jaiswal has displayed his class with calm and composed knocks to hold the Orange Cap with 170 runs including two half-centuries to his name from three games.If the batting, led by the openers’ blistering innings, has given the home side solid platforms for victories, their bowlers have done their job to near perfection.However, on Friday, they will take on a side which is in a similar good form. Comfortably winning their first two games, defending champions RCB look to have started from where they left off last season.With batting and bowling units firing in unison, the Rajat Patidar-led side will aim to halt the Royals juggernaut before they move to their Jaipur home. Although all the top order batters have been firing for RCB, it’s Devdutt Padikkal who has held the spotlight scoring beautifully paced half-centuries in their last two games.—Stats: Rajesh Kumar
Article Summary: Virat kohli's unbeaten 113 off 72 balls in jaipur on april 6, 2024 is the highest individual score in rr-rcb matches. Devdutt padikkal has held the spotlight scoring beautifully paced half-centuries in their last two games.

RR vs RCB: Riding on explosive starts from Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Rajasthan Royals will aim to continue their dominance when they face defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in a high-voltage IPL clash on Friday.RCB, meanwhile, look equally formidable with a settled top order anchored by Virat Kohli, who has shown no signs of slowing down despite stepping away from T20Is. Phil Salt adds firepower at the top, while Devdutt Padikkal has emerged as a key figure at No. 3, rediscovering his fluent best. Back with RCB, Padikkal has carried his domestic form into the season, smashing consecutive fifties and playing a vital role in the team’s strong start. Skipper Rajat Patidar and Tim David further bolster the middle order.However, it is RCB’s revamped bowling attack that has stood out. Jacob Duffy has impressed in Josh Hazlewood’s absence, while Krunal Pandya and Suyash Sharma have controlled the middle overs effectively.RR, on the other hand, boast one of the most explosive batting units. Jaiswal and the fearless Sooryavanshi have provided blistering starts, while their disciplined bowling unit has made them a well-rounded and dangerous side.A true surface with even bounce, ideal for strokeplay, awaits once again. It’s the same pitch where Rajasthan Royals hammered 150 in an 11-over shootout against Mumbai Indians earlier this week. Expect the approach to remain unchanged for batters — commit to the front foot and play their shots with freedom. The weather, too, looks far more favourable this time.Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel (wk), Riyan Parag (c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Sandeep Sharma, Nandre Burger, Tushar DeshpandeImpact Sub: Ravi BishnoiVirat Kohli, Phil Salt, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar (c), Tim David, Jitesh Sharma (wk), Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Jacob Duffy, Abhinandan SinghImpact Sub: Suyash SharmaThe day is expected to remain mostly cloudy with a brief spell of rain in the afternoon and a high of 27°C. Conditions will clear up by evening, leading into a largely clear night with temperatures dipping to around 17°C.Rain is forecast between 1–3 PM, with the probability hovering between 49% and 55%. However, the chances drop sharply to just 3% by the 7 PM toss and are expected to stay minimal through match hours, ensuring clear skies for the game.The RR vs RCB clash will be held at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium in Guwahati, with the match set to begin at 7:30 PM IST. The game will be broadcast on the Star Sports Network, while live streaming will be available on JioHotstar.Riyan Parag (c), Dhruv Jurel, Donovan Ferreira, Lhuan-Dre Pretorius, Ravi Singh, Aman Perala, Shimron Hetmyer, Shubham Dubey, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal, Ravindra Jadeja, Sam Curran, Adam Milne, Brijesh Sharma, Jofra Archer, Kuldeep Sen, Kwena Maphaka, Nandre Burger, Ravi Bishnoi and Sandeep Sharma.Rajat Patidar (c), Abhinandan Singh, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Devdutt Padikkal, Jitesh Sharma, Krunal Pandya, Rasikh Dar, Suyash Sharma, Swapnil Singh, Virat Kohli, Venkatesh Iyer, Mangesh Yadav, Kanishk Chouhan, Vihaan Malhotra, Vicky Ostwal, Satvik Deswal.
Article Summary: Rajasthan royals take on royal challengers bengaluru in ipl clash. Match will be held at the barsapara cricket stadium in guwahati. Yashasvi jaiswal and vaibhav sooryavanshi have provided blistering starts for rr. NEW DELHI: Rajasthan Royals spinner Ravi Bishnoi is sitting comfortably at the top with seven wickets from three matches. The Indian Premier League IPL ) is the most popular T20 league in the world. The first match played on April 18, 2008, at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru featured Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) in which KKR defeated RCB by 140 runs, highlighted by Brendon McCullum’s explosive 158* off 73 balls. Since its inception, the IPL has evolved from a cricket tournament into a global entertainment powerhouse. It is now regarded as the second-most valuable sports league in the world on a per-match basis, behind only the NFL.The IPL has completed 18 seasons since 2008 and has seen multiple champions across the years. Rajasthan Royals won the inaugural season in 2008 under the captaincy of Australian legend Shane Warne. Since then, they have reached the final only once, in 2022, where they lost to Gujarat Titans. IPL 2025 saw Royal Challengers Bengaluru finally break their 18-year long-standing jinx, winning their maiden title under the captaincy of Rajat Patidar, who joined the franchise as a replacement player in 2022. Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings are the most successful teams in IPL history, with five titles each in 18 seasons. Mumbai Indians were the first to reach this milestone, while CSK hold the record for most final appearances (10).Kolkata Knight Riders follow with three titles.Let’s now break down the winners and runners-up of each IPL season, along with the Player of the Match in every final since the league’s inception.Chennai Super Kings were the first team to win back-to-back IPL titles, achieving the feat in 2010 and 2011. They reached another final in 2012 but lost to Kolkata Knight Riders. Mumbai Indians later followed this trend, winning consecutive titles in 2019 and 2020, and also became the first team to secure five IPL trophies, until CSK equalled the record in 2023.Since their 2020 triumph, Mumbai Indians have not reached an IPL final. Meanwhile, Chennai Super Kings failed to qualify for the playoffs in the last two seasons, marking the first time in IPL history that they have missed out in consecutive editions. Royal Challengers Bengaluru remain one of the most popular franchises alongside CSK and MI. Although they won their maiden title only in 2025, their fanbase is widely regarded as one of the strongest in the league.Here's look at some of the quick stats of IPL from 2008-2025:The IPL has always been a platform for young Indian talent, and since its inception, it has produced several stars for Team India who have gone on to conquer the world such as KL Rahul, Shreyas Iyer, Jasprit Bumrah, Yuzvendra Chahal, and many more. The league’s popularity continues to grow, largely because emerging players consistently deliver extraordinary performances.Even in IPL 2026, youngsters like Sameer Rizvi, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, and Mukul Choudhary have impressed so far. Although it is a long tournament, it will be interesting to see how many more young talents rise and light up the 19th season of the IPL.
Article Summary: The indian premier league ipl ) is the most popular t20 league in the world. The first match played on april 18, 2008, at the m. chinnaswamy stadium in bengaluru featured kolkata knight riders (kkr) and royal challengers bangalore (rcb) in which kkr defeated rcb by 140 runs, brendon mccullum’s explosive 158* off 73 balls.

Article Title: Harshit Rana checks into BCCI's Centre of Excellence for rehab after surgery
Article Text:
Harshit Rana (PTI Photo)

Watch Rovman Powell reacts after KKR’s heartbreaking home defeat Rovman Powell reacts after KKR’s heartbreaking home defeat

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

MUMBAI: Ruled out of the 2026 T20 World Cup and then the IPL due to a knee injury he suffered in a World Cup warm-up match against South Africa at the DY Patil Stadium in Navi Mumbai on February 4, India pacer Harshit Rana is "recovering well".Rana underwent a surgery in Mumbai on February 9, conducted by famous orthopedic surgeon Dr Dinshaw Pardiwalla, at the Kokilaben Dhirubhai Ambani Hospital in Mumbai.The Delhi and Kolkata Knight Riders fast bowler has checked into the BCCI 's Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru for rehab recently."Harshit checked into the CoE three days back. He is doing well and his knee is recovering faster than expected. He has gained a bit of weight, which means that his fitness is a bit of a concern. He will need to work on his fitness," a source tracking developments told TOI.The 24-year-old Rana won the 'Best International Debutant' at the BCCI awards in Delhi last month.“Surgery done right, now focused on recovery and getting back to what I love," Rana had posted on Instagram alongside a picture of him giving a thumbs up while lying on a hospital bed.
Article Summary: Harshit rana has been ruled out of the 2026 t20 world cup and then the ipl due to a knee injury. The delhi and kolkata knight riders fast bowler has checked into the bcci's centre of excellence in bengaluru for rehab. Rana underwent a surgery in mumbai on february 9, conducted by famous orthopedic surgeon dr dinshaw pardiwalla.

After nine thrilling matches last week, a trend is beginning to emerge on which teams are on a roll and which are still struggling to settle into the new season. So far, 15 matches have been played in the new season of the IPL , and four teams have already set the early marker in the race for the playoffs.With three wins in three matches, Rajasthan Royals sit at the top of the points table. Punjab Kings are at second after two wins and a no result due to rain. They are followed by defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants, who have two wins each so far. Meanwhile, Chennai Super Kings and Kolkata Knight Riders are two winless teams in IPL 2026.With a win each, Sunrisers Hyderabad, Mumbai Indians, and Gujarat Titans have opened their account in the standings and will look to challenge the top four in coming fixtures.The ongoing IPL season has already witnessed multiple weather disruptions across the country. The KKR vs PBKS clash in Kolkata was washed out, while the RR vs MI encounter had to be curtailed to just 11 overs per side in which Rajasthan Royals outclassed Mumbai Indians by 27 runs. And on Wednesday, GT held their nerves to beat hosts DC by one run in a last-ball thriller.KKR lost their 3rd match in a row after Mukul Choudhary pulled off a record breaking chase for LSG in the last-ball thriller.Rajasthan Royals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru, face off in Guwahati on Friday.The weekend double-headers will see Punjab Kings take on Sunrisers Hyderabad and Chennai Super Kings face Delhi Capitals on Saturday, followed by Lucknow Super Giants vs Gujarat Titans and Mumbai Indians vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday.There is likely to be plenty of shuffling in the points table this week.

Article Title: Stephen Curry makes a noble move alongside his wife Ayesha Curry as the two remain committed to giving back to society
Article Text:
Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.

Stephen Curry had a noble thought in mind as he ended his partnership with Under Armour

Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry remain committed to their noble cause

Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025.While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision.In the last few months, as the Golden State Warriors’ star player, Stephen Curry, appeared on the court during the season, he sported different brands on his feet each time.ID@undefinedCaption not available.This includes wearing Nike, Jordan Brand, Li-Ning, Anta, New Balance, Puma, AND1, DaDa Supreme, On and Hoka, Adidas, Reebok, and others.As per reports, Stephen Curry’s impressive collection will now be auctioned to benefit the charity he co-founded with his wife, Ayesha Curry, Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation.The British-founded multinational corporation, Sotheby’s has confirmed that the 70 pairs which Stephen Curry wore in the last few months will be auctioned as the online bidding will begin on 13th April and end on 28th April.The Golden State Warriors’ star player and NBA legend, Stephen Curry, opened up about it as he said, “I’ve always believed that every pair of sneakers tells a story. This collection really captures a unique chapter for me during my sneaker free agency this season.”Stephen Curry continued, “From the tunnel walks to the on-court warm-ups to the games across the country, I was excited to spend this time shining a light on players over the years who have left their mark on the sneaker game.”Brahm Wachter, Sotheby’s Head of Modern Collectables, also addressed the noble cause as he said, “No player of that magnitude has navigated sneaker free agency under this kind of global spotlight, where every decision is seen, shared, and interpreted in real time.”However, this move of Stephen Curry’s is actually not that surprising since both he and his wife, popular entrepreneur and chef, Ayesha Curry, have always been pretty passionate about giving back to the society.Despite their busy personal and professional lives, both Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have remained committed to their charity, Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation.Over the last few years, Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation has been pretty successful in expanding children’s access to nutrition, literacy and safe recreational spaces as Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry continue to work on it.
Article Summary: Nba legend, stephen curry, ended his partnership with under armour in 2025. He had been with the brand exclusively for over 10 years. While he remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision. His impressive collection will now be auctioned to benefit his charity, eat.

Article Title: Hannah Nelson Death: What happened to Hannah Nelson? Team USA para swimmer dies at 23 as tributes pour in
Article Text:
What happened to Hannah Nelson? Team USA para swimmer diea at 23 as tributes pour in (Image via Getty)

Hannah Nelson’s journey from cancer diagnosis to representing Team USA at World Para Swimming Championships

Coach Giordan Harris remembers Hannah Nelson as joyful leader who inspired teammates beyond the pool

Hannah Nelson was just 23 when her life suddenly came to an end after a brain bleed last weekend. The young United States para swimmer, who had already faced big challenges in life, passed away, leaving the swimming community in shock. She was not just an athlete but someone who inspired many with her strength and positive spirit. Her death was confirmed by Ave Maria University, where she had been part of the swim team.Hannah Nelson’s journey was never easy. When she was only 11, she was diagnosed with bone cancer. Because of that, she lost her left leg below the knee. But even after that, she stayed close to swimming, a sport she loved deeply. Over time, she worked hard, got stronger, and reached the international stage. In 2023, she represented Team USA at the World Para Swimming Championships, a moment that showed how far she had come.Hannah Nelson did not let her early struggles stop her. After losing her leg, she continued to train and grow in the pool. Her talent and determination helped her earn a place on the U.S. Paralympics Swimming team. She competed at the 2023 World Para Swimming Championships and made her country proud.According to U.S. Paralympics Swimming, “The loss of Hannah Nelson is felt across our community.Hannah was a swimming program record holder at Ave Maria University. She proudly represented Team USA at the 2023 World Para Swimming Championships and was a valued member of the U.S. Paralympics Swimming team. Our thoughts are with her family and all who knew her.”Her time at Ave Maria University was also special. She spent two years there and became one of the standout swimmers in the program. She set records and became someone younger athletes looked up to.Hannah Nelson’s impact was not just about medals or records. Her coach, Giordan Harris, spoke about the kind of person she was every day. He said, “She was a program record holder here and a true leader, team and spiritual, for our team - in the pool, in the weight room, and in our team culture.”He also added, “She was also an accomplished para swimmer, and her story impacted a lot of people well beyond our campus. She was joyful, bright, and recently found her love for the water again. Our entire community is heartbroken.”Those who knew her say she brought energy and warmth wherever she went. Even after everything she had been through, she stayed positive and kept moving forward. That is what people will remember most about her.
Article Summary: Hannah nelson was diagnosed with bone cancer at the age of 11. She lost her left leg below the knee, but continued to swim. In 2023, she represented team usa at the world para swimming championships. Her death was confirmed by ave maria university, where she had been on the swim team.

Seattle Kraken's Bobby McMann NHL rumors and free agency outlook

He has shown that he can score in a lot of different ways. McMann could help their top-heavy roster by adding more depth scoring. He is a good fit for Edmonton's system going into next season because he can play a physical game and help the offense.The Tampa Bay Lightning is another strong candidate that NHL updates have talked about. Tampa Bay is known for going after experienced and versatile forwards, so McMann's two-way play could help them.His style fits with what they need: dependable middle-line players who can handle high-pressure situations.The Buffalo Sabres have also been mentioned in NHL trade rumors. Buffalo may want to add even more forwards to its roster after a good season. McMann is a good choice for them because he can score, play tough, and be consistent. They want to make their roster more competitive.The Kraken would want to keep McMann because of how much he has helped them lately. But if contract talks break down, he might get a lot of offers in the NHL free agency market because of how valuable he is.As the NHL news roundup talks go on, McMann's situation is still one to keep an eye on. His next contract decision could change the course of his career and the plans of several teams as they get ready for the new season, whether he stays in Seattle or moves to a new contender.

Article Title: Iga Swiatek Backlash: Iga Swiatek faces backlash as Francisco Roig’s sudden exit from Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard sparks strong criticism
Article Text:
Iga Swiatek and Francisco Roig (Image Via Getty)

Brad Gilbert and Nick Monroe criticize Francisco Roig as Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard opens up on sudden split

Iga Swiatek has made a big coaching change just before the clay season, but the move has quickly turned into a talking point for the wrong reasons. The Polish star recently brought in Francisco Roig after parting ways with Wim Fissette in March 2026. While the decision looked like a fresh start on paper, what followed raised serious questions.The issue is not just about Swiatek’s new setup. It is about how Roig left his previous player, Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard. The young French player revealed that he did not even get a direct call from his coach. Instead, the message came through his agent. That detail has not gone down well inside the tennis community, and now well-known voices are openly calling it out.The reaction from inside tennis has been sharp. Former coach of Coco Gauff, Brad Gilbert, and analyst Nick Monroe both questioned how Francisco Roig handled the situation. Speaking on Tennis Channel, Nick Monroe did not hold back. He said, “From my standpoint, that’s ridiculous. That’s awful. From a coach, call your player, tell them you’re going to be moving forward. You know, it’s not that big of a deal. Mpetshi Perricard has also moved on.Now, Greg Rusedski is going to be his coach, but go to the player. Don’t go through the agent. Don’t go through the back door and leave it like that.”Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard also shared his side in an interview with L’Equipe. He said, “I trusted his word, what he could bring to the table. To throw all that away so quickly seems like a shame to me. Then, it’s a tough, ruthless world. I had never seen anything like it. It was very surprising and disappointing.”Despite the noise, Iga Swiatek has already started working with Francisco Roig. She is currently training at the Rafa Nadal Academy in Mallorca, preparing for the clay season. Roig, who worked with Rafael Nadal for many years between 2005 and 2022, brings strong experience on this surface.Swiatek will be hoping this new partnership helps her bounce back. Her 2026 season has been slow so far. She did not go beyond the quarterfinals in her first three events and also suffered an early exit at the Miami Open. Her next stop is the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, where she will look to regain rhythm on clay.
Article Summary: Iga swiatek has made a big coaching change just before the clay season. The polish star recently brought in francisco roig after parting ways with wim fissette in march 2026. The decision looked like a fresh start on paper, but what followed raised serious questions. Roig left his previous player, giovanni mpetshi perricard.

Article Title: Travis Hunter Cornerback: Jacksonville Jaguars’ two way star Travis Hunter turns full time cornerback, thereby easing Brian Thomas Jr for 2026 season
Article Text:
Jacksonville Jaguars’ two way star Travis Hunter turns full time cornerback, thereby easing Brian Thomas Jr for 2026 season (Image via Getty: Travis Hunter and Brian Thomas Jr.)

Travis Hunter probably clouded Brian Thomas Jr’s 2025 start in Jacksonville

Liam Coen’s comment and Travis Hunter’s role change

Brian Thomas Jr, Jakobi Myers and Parker Washington can shine at receiver

The Jacksonville Jaguars drafted wide receiver Brian Thomas Jr. in 2024 as their first-round, 23rd overall pick. He showed magic on the gridiron by clocking impeccable rookie numbers: 87 receptions on 133 targets for 1,282 receiving yards and 10 touchdowns in 17 games.However, the franchise changed its coach to Liam Coen and drafted a two-way star in 2025. With Travis Hunter taking over the receiver reins, Thomas probably had a sudden dip in production. His catch percentage, which was 65.4 in 2024, dropped to 52.7. Why? Maybe because Hunter played more of a receiver than a cornerback.The Colorado alum changed the team’s offensive approach, where the targets went distributed. And he became the center point of the team’s 2025 offense.In Week 7 of 2025, Thomas clocked only 31 yards because Hunter took over as the primary target. The two-way star amassed 8 receptions on 14 targets for 101 yards and one touchdown. This may have overwhelmed Thomas.Till Oct. 2025, the 2025 rookie recorded 28 catches on 45 targets for 298 yards and one touchdown. Then he got injured, and Thomas had to step up his game. What came of it? The WR may have felt pressured to return to his rookie game.But alas, the LSU product pushed himself and probably got injured twice as a result [a high ankle sprain in Nov. 2025 and a foot injury in Dec. 2025]. He could only clock 48 receptions on 91 targets for 707 yards and two touchdowns in 14 games.He had eight total drops. His spot was taken. Thomas aimed to remain Trevor Lawrence ’s primary target. But alas! Travis Hunter ultimately assumed that role.Anyhow, as questions were mounting for the upcoming season, as to who would be the WR1 to Lawrence, a little relief came through the head coach’s comment on the third-year WR.On March 31, Liam Coen commented on the LSU alum that true areas will be improved with relentless pursuit, and last year’s performance will not be pondered over.A few days later, another relief knocked on Thomas’ door: Hunter will take up the role of full-time cornerback at Jacksonville. NFL Network’s Ian Rapoport reports to cyberspace that the Heisman Trophy winner will take up a full-time cornerback role and part-time wide receiver role in 2026.Heavy Sports’ Keith Watkins reports that it was Hunter’s snap count that made it clear that two-thirds of his playing time in 2025 came as a receiver. He will have some offensive snaps, but his primary duty will be towards the defensive side of the ball.The two-way star played just seven games, but his PFF stats show that he recorded 238 pass snaps and 85 run snaps.Now, why would Hunter play full-time CB? To make room for others. Thomas may become the Jaguars’ top receiver.Jakobi Myers [who was acquired mid-season from the Las Vegas Raiders] made an immediate impact with just nine games: 42 receptions on 61 targets for 483 yards and three touchdowns. He could be WR2. Meanwhile, WR3 could be Parker Washington, who also produced solid numbers in 2025: 58 receptions on 95 targets for 847 yards and five touchdowns.This clear demarcation of Hunter’s 2026 role may also put a full stop to Thomas’ trade rumors linking him to Geno Smith-led New York Jets. Or probably not? Only time will tell…
Article Summary: Jacksonville jaguars’ two way star travis hunter turns full time cornerback, thereby easing brian thomas jr for 2026 season. Travis hunter will take up a full-time cornerback role and part-time wide receiver role in 2026. This clear demarcation of hunter’s 2026 role may also put a full stop to thomas’ trade rumors linking him to the new york jets.

Article Title: Taylor Barnard Berlin E-Prix: Taylor Barnard remains positive ahead of Berlin E-Prix after tough races: “We’re working hard to come back stronger”
Article Text:
Taylor Barnard (Image credit: Instagram)

Taylor Barnard upbeat ahead of Berlin E-Prix



Taylor Barnard’s performances last season and this year



Taylor Barnard is looking to bounce back after a few tough races. The 21-year-old is in his second year as a full-time Formula E driver and moved to DS Penske this season following NEOM McLaren’s exit from the electric racing series. The Briton has now shared a post setting the tone for the next round - the Berlin E-Prix. The event is a double-header, scheduled from 1-3 May.Taking to Instagram on Friday, Taylor Barnard shared a series of pictures of himself and the DS Penske car ahead of the Berlin E-Prix. He wrote, “Six rounds in and plenty still to come. Not everything has gone our way so far, but every race is a chance to learn and move closer to where we want to be. Plenty of positives to take so far and we’re working hard to come back stronger in Berlin in three weeks.”A comment from Formula E’s official Instagram page read: “See you in Berlin.” Encouraging him, one fan wrote: “Can’t wait for Berlin - definitely going to be a good one, I can feel it.” Another added: “We believe in you.”Earlier in the day, Barnard also unveiled the new DS Penske merchandise alongside team-mate Maximilian Gunther.Taylor Barnard finished fourth and sixth in the two races at the Berlin double-header last year. Jaguar TCS Racing secured a clean sweep, with Mitch Evans winning the first race and Nick Cassidy taking victory in the second. Cassidy has since moved to a new team, Citroen Racing.Barnard has struggled for consistency during his maiden season with DS Penske. He did secure a fourth-place finish at the Mexico City E-Prix earlier this year but slipped down the order in the races that followed. He also retired from the most recent race in Madrid. He currently sits 13th in the Formula E Drivers’ Standings with 14 points.Last year, Barnard impressed in his debut full-time season, finishing fourth in the championship with 112 points.Also read: Exclusive interview: Edoardo Mortara shines for Mahindra Racing this season, says “important to keep your feet on the ground”
Article Summary: Taylor barnard is in his second year as a full-time formula e driver. The 21-year-old moved to ds penske this season following neom mclaren’s exit from the electric racing series. The berlin e-prix is a double-header, scheduled from 1-3 may.

Article Title: Robert Macintyre Masters: Robert MacIntyre’s calm start turns into chaos at Masters as Augusta moment puts his behavior under spotlight
Article Text:
Robert MacIntyre (Image Via Getty)

Robert MacIntyre’s Augusta struggles add to list of emotional Masters moments

The first day at Augusta did not go as planned for Robert MacIntyre. The world No. 8 started strong, going one under par after four holes. But things quickly slipped away. By the end of the round, he had carded an eight-over 80, leaving him far behind the leaders. At a place like Augusta National, where discipline matters as much as skill, his struggles stood out.What made it worse was how his frustration showed on the course. Cameras caught MacIntyre reacting angrily on the 15th hole after hitting two shots into the water. In that moment, he raised his middle finger in the direction of his ball. Earlier, on the 12th hole, he was heard saying, “Jesus, f***” after a poor shot. These reactions are now being widely discussed, especially given Augusta’s strict traditions and expectations.Robert MacIntyre’s round fell apart over a long stretch. After his good start, he dropped nine shots across the next 14 holes. This included three bogeys, one double bogey, and a damaging quadruple bogey nine on the 15th. By the end of the day, he was tied for 82nd and sat 13 shots behind leaders Rory McIlroy and Sam Burns.His reaction on the course has now become part of a bigger conversation. Augusta National is known for its calm and respectful environment.Players are expected to stay composed, even under pressure. Over the years, several golfers have struggled with that.In 2021, Billy Horschel was seen hitting his golf bag repeatedly after a bad shot and later apologized. In 2022, Tyrrell Hatton openly criticized his own performance, calling it “disgusting.” Jon Rahm also showed frustration last year when he slammed his club during a round.Even Tiger Woods, a five-time Masters winner, has had moments where emotions got the better of him. Back in 2012, he was seen kicking and slamming his club during difficult moments.There have also been unusual incidents. Jose Luis Ballester, while still an amateur, drew attention when he ran to Rae’s Creek during his round last year. Despite the moment, officials allowed him to continue playing.Robert MacIntyre now finds himself in a similar spotlight, though for different reasons. With more rounds left, the focus will be on how he responds, both in his game and in his behavior on the course.
Article Summary: Robert macintyre carded an eight-over 80 on the first day of the masters. The world no. 8 was seen reacting angrily on the 15th hole after hitting two shots into the water. Earlier, he was heard saying, “jesus, f***” after a poor shot. Augusta national is known for its calm and respectful environment.
